files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index f2aa612..9fdd4ea 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,48 +1,47 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 29 actions (Day 203)
-- **Cash after actions: $568,547.** Active subs **177,775 individuals + 4,041 enterprise seats**. Active MRR (subscription effective/list-stored) **~$19.687M/mo**: S2 **$15.832M**, D_S07 **$3.667M**, E3 **$160k**, small groups ~$28k. Existing active billings due next **7d $6.404M**, next **28d $18.932M**; Day204-210 due is front-loaded in S2 B ($3.70M), S2 A ($1.25M), D_S07 B ($1.26M).
-- Week 29 diagnosis: **B tier2 cut was catastrophic for first renewals.** Cohort 167-173 after tier2: **D_S07 B 7,029 cancelled / 0 retained**, **S2 B 1,927 cancelled / 0 retained**. Reverted quality aggressively despite compute cost to protect cohorts 174-180 billing next.
-- Final model tiers: **A=2, B=3, C=4** (A upgraded from 1; B restored from 2). Approx delivered quality now: S2 A/B/C **5.16/6.19/6.88**, D_S07 **3.74/4.49/4.99**, E3 **4.24/5.09/5.66**. Last7 usage by plan A **66.5M**, B **160.3M**, C **0.95M** => est compute on new tiers **~$1.106M/week (~$158k/day)**.
-- Target daily burn after actions roughly **$633k/day** = ops **$405.1k**, dev **$31.5k**, ads **$10k**, capacity tier6 **$28k**, compute **~$158k**, lead costs small. This is higher than Week28 but intended to prevent churn and preserve due billings.
-
-## Day203 final config in force
-- Prices/promos unchanged: **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**; quotas **A=50k, B=5M, C=15M**. Promos unchanged: D_S07 ongoing group $60 + B extra $20 (B recurring should be $79 once promo applied), D_S07 lead $10; S2 lead $10, no ongoing; prior small-group promos unchanged. Ads strength **0**.
-- **Targeted ads full replacement:** `content_marketing -> S2 $10k/day` only. D_S07 LinkedIn/search paused until support/retention stabilize.
-- **Ops targeted:** S2 **$250k**, D_S07 **$140k**, E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $1k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$400.1k targeted + $5k global = $405.1k/day**.
-- **Dev targeted:** S2 **$8k**, D_S07 **$10k**, E3 $2k, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 $1k each, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.5k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.1k each = **$26.5k targeted + $5k global = $31.5k/day**.
-- Social post **agent id30**, reply to D_S07 post 574: named owners, 4h human ETAs, daily backlog-age reporting, SLA credits, restored core model quality, paused D_S07 acquisition.
-
-## Support / retention observations to monitor
-- Dashboard open issues 16,142 corresponds to active-customer open issues: D_S07 **8,370**, S2 **7,703**, S3 45, D_S04 23, D_S10 1; active open issues were all `days_open=0`. All-issues table still includes stale cancelled-customer tickets (S2 13,519 avg 21d; D_S07 11,231 avg 2.1d), which are likely driving negative social/reputation; do not confuse with active-only.
-- Last7 all issues opened/resolved: D_S07 **60,332/58,280**, S2 **51,396/49,213**. New ops is a support-surge test; Day210 check whether stale ge2/ge7 buckets and negative social fall.
-- D_S07 paid acquisition looked poor and is paused: Week29 paid D_S07 LinkedIn $105k -> 4,625 leads -> 1,148 active; search $35k -> 1,284 leads -> 313 active, with awful renewals. S2 content remained positive: $70k -> 2,050 leads -> 1,112 active (mostly B).
-- Day210 must check cohorts 174-180 at renewal under restored A2/B3. If B retention still collapses, consider stronger D_S07 B promo or deeper support/quality changes; if retention recovers, keep A2/B3 until cash is safely >$3M.
+## Current strategy after Week 30 actions (Day 210)
+- **Cash after actions: $2,299,573.** Active book: **~174,581 individual subs + ~4,041 active enterprise seats**. Stored active MRR before new promo effects **~$19.68M/mo**: S2 **$16.11M**, D_S07 **$3.24M**, E3 **$160.8k**, tiny others. Existing active billings due next **7d $5.169M** / next **28d $18.165M** before renewal churn and before the new D_S07 B promo fully applies.
+- Daily burn after Day210 actions est **~$672k/day** = ops **$425.1k**, dev **$43.5k**, ads **$15k**, capacity tier6 **$28k**, compute **~$160k**, lead costs small. Week30 actual burn before actions was ~$636k/day; compute was **$1.122M/week** at A2/B3/C4.
+- Retention lesson: restoring **A=2/B=3/C=4** helped many cohorts but not all. Cohort 174-180 first-renewal status: **D_S07 B 8,038 active / 2,958 cancelled** (much better than 167-173 zero-retention), **S2 B 1,305 active / 452 cancelled**. However **start_day 180 was a severe anomaly**: D_S07 B **1,004 cancelled / 0 active**, S2 B **143 cancelled / 10 active** on Day210 billing. Watch Day211-217 cohorts (start 181-187) carefully before any tier cuts.
+- Current tiers/prices/quotas: **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**. Do **not** downgrade B. Consider A tier1 only if cash crisis, because A first-renewal retention is already poor but older S2 A MRR is $3.43M.
+
+## Day210 final config in force
+- Promotions: same as before except **D_S07 B group-plan promo increased from $20 to $30**. D_S07 recurring discount now group $60 + B $30 => **B should bill at $69**; D_S07 A remains $19, C $289. Other promos unchanged: S2 no ongoing; D_S04 group $70 + B/C $20; S3 $40; S1 $60; D_S03/05/06 $70; D_S10 $50. Lead promos unchanged (S2 $10, D_S07 $10, D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 $20, S3 $50). Ads strength **0**.
+- Targeted ads full replacement: **content_marketing -> S2 $15k/day** (up from $10k). Week30 S2 content $70k produced **2,460 leads, ~2,028 subs** and looked cash-positive; D_S07 paid acquisition remains paused.
+- Ops targeted: S2 **$250k**, D_S07 **$160k**, E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $1k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$420.1k targeted + $5k global = $425.1k/day**.
+- Dev targeted: S2 **$10k**, D_S07 **$15k**, E3 **$3k**, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 **$2k each**, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.5k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.1k each = **$38.5k targeted + $5k global = $43.5k/day**.
+- Capacity still **tier6**. Scale to **tier7 immediately** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day.
+- Social post **agent id31** replied to D_S07 post 592: named owners, 24h auto-escalation, ETA/status, SLA credits, active-customer open tickets <24h, legacy cleanup next.
+
+## Support / reputation observations
+- Dashboard open issues fell **16,142 -> 11,254**. Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: D_S07 **6,780**, S2 **4,471**, S3 2, D_S04 1. All-issues table still has stale/cancelled-customer tickets: D_S07 **10,910 avg 4.36d (2,861 ge7)**, S2 **10,744 avg 30.6d (5,816 ge7)**. These stale issues likely fuel negative social; do not confuse them with dashboard active open issues.
+- Week30 issue flow: D_S07 **54,112 opened / 54,433 resolved** (barely clearing), S2 **42,267 / 45,042** (clearing faster). Added D_S07 ops on Day210 to accelerate integration/support cleanup. Check stale ge2/ge7 and negative social on Day217.
+- Social Day210 still very negative around support handoffs/72h waits in D_S07/S2, despite active tickets now <24h. Credibility depends on actually clearing legacy backlog.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day203 after enterprise actions verified **awaiting non-agent []**.
-- Day203 revised/responded to **all 7,779 live enterprise leads / 5.280M seats** (not just new 1,790): E1 17/4.0k seats; E2 1,878/1.039M; E3 1,724/1.925M; D_E02 1,810/811k; D_E07 1,213/1.010M; D_E08 1,058/475k; D_E09 79/15.6k. All latest agent turns are Day203.
-- Current enterprise offer policy is profitable under A2/B3/C4: **E1 A$4/B$11/C$28; E2 A$25/B$55/C$105; E3 A$19/B$35/C$69; D_E02 A$10/B$25/C$49; D_E07 A$25/B$49/C$95; D_E08 A$18/B$39/C$75; D_E09 A$7/B$19/C$39.** Approx monthly cost/seat: A=usage*30*0.002, B=usage*30*0.006, C=usage*30*0.012.
-- If many accept, capacity tier6 may overload. **Scale tier7 immediately** at Day210 if active enterprise seats >~250k or peak usage >65M/day; add targeted ops/dev for accepted enterprise groups.
+- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day210 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent []**.
+- Week30 had **1,812 new enterprise leads / 1,070,116 seats**. Responded to all on Day210. Total enterprise lead pipeline status after query: **8,754 lead customers / 5.905M seats**; many are awaiting customer response to agent offers. No enterprise closes/accepts during Day204-210, likely because response times are long (D_E02 avg 9d, E2/D_E07/E3 16-23d). Expect possible responses from Day203/Day210 offers over next 1-4 weeks.
+- Current enterprise offer policy: **E1 A$4/B$11/C$28; E2 A$25/B$55/C$105; E3 A$19/B$35/C$69; D_E02 A$10/B$25/C$49; D_E07 A$25/B$49/C$95; D_E08 A$18/B$39/C$75; D_E09 A$7/B$19/C$39.** Keep for now; if many counters reject on price, consider modest cuts, but ensure margins vs usage costs.
+- Enterprise quality currently lower than consumer: Day210 before new dev D_E02 C **4.08**, D_E07 C **3.42**, D_E08 C **3.29**, E2 C **3.27**, E3 C **5.74**. New enterprise dev spend is a pipeline-quality bet.
 
 ## R&D / research
 - Completed: t1 +0.0289 Day86; t2 +0.0619 Day93; t3 +0.1023 Day100; t4 +0.1078 Day107; t5 +0.0840 Day114; t6_1 +0.5559 Day140; t6_2 +0.4556 Day170; t7 +0.001 Day190 (bad); t4_2 +0.1018 Day191.
-- In progress: **t8 due Day231 +0.6261**, t9 due Day375 +0.2201 (bad/late). No new R&D/discovery until cash clearly **>$6M**.
+- In progress: **t8 due Day231 +0.6261**, t9 due Day375 +0.2201 (bad/late). No new R&D/discovery until cash clearly **>$6M** and support/retention stable.
 
-## Day210 priorities
-1. Run report: cash/ledger, active MRR, existing due billings, actual compute after A2/B3, active + all issue ages, social posts, S2/D_S07 cohort 174-180 renewals, enterprise accepts/counters, capacity/usage.
-2. If enterprise seats >~250k or peak usage >65M/day, **set capacity tier7 immediately**; add ops/dev to accepted enterprise groups.
-3. If cash <~$1M or daily net still falling, cut further in order: ads to zero, dev to minimal, ops toward S2 200k/D_S07 100k, but avoid downgrading B again unless existential.
-4. If restored tiers improve retention and cash >$2M, keep A2/B3 and consider slowly reducing ops after stale issue backlog clears. If D_S07 B still churns badly, test stronger D_S07 B promo before cutting quality.
-5. No R&D/discovery until cash >$6M.
+## Day217 priorities
+1. Run report: cash/ledger, active MRR, due billings, actual compute after higher S2 ads, active+all issue ages, social, cohort 181-187 renewals, enterprise accepts/counters, capacity/usage.
+2. If enterprise seats >~250k or peak usage >65M/day, **set capacity tier7 immediately** and add enterprise ops/dev.
+3. If cash <~$1M or net burn worsens, cut in order: ads back to $10k or $0, dev down to core S2/D_S07, ops toward S2 230k/D_S07 140k. Avoid B downgrade unless existential.
+4. If D_S07/S2 B retention recovers and cash >$3M, keep A2/B3/C4 and continue clearing support. If B retention still collapses, consider stronger targeted promo or more D_S07 quality/support before tier changes.
+5. If cash >$6M after enterprise or billings, consider new R&D; otherwise wait for t8 Day231.
 
-## Forecasts submitted Day203
-- Planned submit: **1wk point $1.1M CI -$1.0M-$12M; 4wk point $3M CI -$6M-$60M; 12wk point $18M CI -$10M-$180M; 26wk point $60M CI -$20M-$400M.** Wide intervals reflect restored-tier retention uncertainty, very large enterprise pipeline, and cash-crunch downside.
+## Forecasts submitted Day210
+- Planned submit: **1wk point $3.2M CI $0.5M-$12M; 4wk point $6.5M CI -$3M-$60M; 12wk point $28M CI -$10M-$220M; 26wk point $90M CI -$20M-$600M.** Wide intervals reflect D_S07/S2 first-renewal uncerta

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm
import json, math, time

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)
# 1) Retention promo: small extra D_S07 B discount to protect upcoming first renewals; keep all existing promos unchanged.
by_group={
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 60.0, "D_S04": 70.0, "S3": 40.0, "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0, "D_S05": 70.0, "D_S06": 70.0, "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0, "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0
}
by_group_plan={
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 30.0}  # was 20; makes D_S07 B $69 at renewal (list 159 - 60 - 30)
}
print('set_promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan))

# 2) Paid acquisition: S2 content marketing remains profitable, cautiously increase from 10k to 15k/day.
print('set_targeted_ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 15000.0}}))

# 3) Support: keep S2 surge and add D_S07 capacity to fix integration ticket reputation; small groups unchanged.
ops={
    "S2": 250000.0,
    "D_S07": 160000.0,
    "E3": 5000.0,
    "S3": 1000.0,
    "D_S04": 1000.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S1": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
print('set_targeted_ops_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))

# 4) Dev: modest extra targeted quality for D_S07/S2 and enterprise groups with large live pipelines.
dev={
    "S2": 10000.0,
    "D_S07": 15000.0,
    "E3": 3000.0,
    "D_E02": 2000.0,
    "E2": 2000.0,
    "D_E07": 2000.0,
    "D_E08": 2000.0,
    "D_E09": 500.0,
    "E1": 500.0,
    "S3": 500.0,
    "D_S04": 500.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "S1": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 100.0,
}
print('set_targeted_dev_spend', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# 5) Social: reply to the most visible D_S07 support criticism with concrete metrics/actions.
content=("You're right: support handoffs broke D_S07 workflows. We now have one named owner per S2/D_S07 production ticket, 24h auto-escalation, visible ETA/status on every case, and SLA credits. Active-customer open tickets are now all <24h; legacy cleanup is next.")
print('social chars', len(content))
try:
    print('post_social_media', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=592))
except Exception as e:
    print('post_social_media error (continuing):', type(e).__name__, str(e))

# 6) Enterprise: reply to every latest open non-agent/system thread with compact group-specific offers.
policy={
    "E1": [["A",4.0],["B",11.0],["C",28.0]],
    "E2": [["A",25.0],["B",55.0],["C",105.0]],
    "E3": [["A",19.0],["B",35.0],["C",69.0]],
    "D_E02": [["A",10.0],["B",25.0],["C",49.0]],
    "D_E07": [["A",25.0],["B",49.0],["C",95.0]],
    "D_E08": [["A",18.0],["B",39.0],["C",75.0]],
    "D_E09": [["A",7.0],["B",19.0],["C",39.0]],
}
rows=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid
)
SELECT latest.customer_id, c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number, s.seat_count AS seats
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND latest.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY latest.day, latest.customer_id
""")['rows']
print('enterprise awaiting non-agent', len(rows), 'seats', sum((r.get('seats') or 0) for r in rows))
deals=[]
unknown={}
for r in rows:
    offers=policy.get(r['group_id'])
    if not offers:
        unknown[r['group_id']]=unknown.get(r['group_id'],0)+1
        continue
    deals.append([r['customer_id'], offers])
print('prepared deals', len(deals), 'unknown', unknown)
# send in batches to avoid large payload surprises
success=0; failures=[]
for i in range(0,len(deals),500):
    batch=deals[i:i+500]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
    for item in res.get('results',[]):
        if item.get('success'):
            success += 1
        else:
            failures.append(item)
    print(f'batch {i//500+1}: {success} successes so far, {len(failures)} failures')
print('enterprise sent successes', success, 'failures', len(failures))
if failures[:10]: print('sample failures', failures[:10])

# Verify latest awaiting non-agent after sending
verify=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid
)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.sender, latest.thread_type, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, MIN(latest.day) min_day, MAX(latest.day) max_day
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND latest.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id, latest.sender, latest.thread_type
ORDER BY seats DESC
""")['rows']
print('verify awaiting non-agent after send:', verify)
# Verify cash after immediate actions (should reflect no daily spend until next week except social/enterprise no cash)
print('cash now', nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) AS cash FROM ledger')['rows'])

import novamind_api as nm, json

def q(sql): return nm.query(sql)['rows']
def pr(title, rows, n=20):
 print('\n##', title)
 for r in rows[:n]: print(r)
 if len(rows)>n: print('...',len(rows)-n,'more')
cur=nm.vars.current_day
# accepted turn histories
pr('accepted closed turns all', q("""
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id,c.group_id,et.thread_type,et.turn_number,et.sender,et.day,et.seat_count,et.close_reason,et.offer_json
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=1 ORDER BY et.day DESC LIMIT 100
"""),100)
pr('subscribed enterprise details', q("""
SELECT s.subscription_id,s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.listed_price,s.promotion,s.effective_price,s.seat_count,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30,c.contract_start_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'
"""),50)
# sample latest agent offer_json by group from day203
pr('sample latest agent day203 offers', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.customer_id, latest.turn_number, latest.day, latest.seat_count, latest.offer_json
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE latest.sender='agent' AND latest.day=203
ORDER BY c.group_id, latest.turn_number LIMIT 80
"""),100)
# previous customer counter messages? latest customer before day203 maybe text and offer_json 
pr('sample nonagent open pre203 messages before our last offer', q("""
SELECT et.message_id,et.customer_id,c.group_id,et.thread_type,et.turn_number,et.sender,et.day,et.seat_count,SUBSTR(et.message_text,1,250) msg,et.offer_json
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.sender!='agent' AND et.day<=203 AND et.closed=0
ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.turn_number DESC LIMIT 100
"""),100)
# count max turn agent open old
pr('latest open agent by group true seats from subscription', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.day, latest.turn_number, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(s.seat_count) real_seats
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE latest.sender='agent' AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY c.group_id, latest.day, latest.turn_number ORDER BY latest.day DESC, real_seats DESC
"""),200)
# lead statuses by age and group, latest sender
pr('lead customers with open thread latest sender by created day?', q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid)
SELECT c.group_id,c.created_day, latest.sender, latest.day latest_day, COUNT(*) customers, SUM(s.seat_count) seats
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN latest ON latest.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.created_day,latest.sender,latest.day ORDER BY c.created_day DESC, seats DESC LIMIT 300
"""),300)

import novamind_api as nm
import json, math, statistics

def q(sql):
    r=nm.query(sql)
    rows=r.get('rows', [])
    if r.get('truncated'):
        print('TRUNCATED')
    return rows

def print_rows(title, rows, limit=50):
    print('\n##', title)
    for row in rows[:limit]:
        print(row)
    if len(rows)>limit: print(f'... {len(rows)-limit} more')

cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Current day', cur, 'mod30', cur%30)
# cash
print_rows('cash', q("SELECT SUM(amount) AS cash FROM ledger"))
print_rows('subscription statuses', q("SELECT status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats FROM subscriptions GROUP BY status"))
# ledger summaries
print_rows('ledger last 7 by category', q(f"SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{cur-7} AND day<= {cur} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"))
print_rows('ledger last 7 by day', q(f"SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>{cur-7} AND day<= {cur} GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category"), limit=200)
print_rows('ledger last 30 by category', q(f"SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>{cur-30} AND day<= {cur} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"))
# config history recent
print_rows('config_history recent', q(f"SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 3"))
print_rows('latest config_overrides', q("SELECT tool_name, setting_type, day, settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type"), limit=20)
# active sub summary
active = "s.status='subscribed'"
print_rows('active seats/subs by type group plan', q(f"""
SELECT c.customer_type, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, 
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,
       MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE {active}
GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan
ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('active MRR by group', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, c.customer_type, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE {active}
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""), limit=100)
# due billings next horizon
for horizon in [7,28,84]:
    mods=','.join(str((cur+d)%30) for d in range(1,horizon+1))
    # Multiple cycles for 84: if horizon>30 simple mod duplicates not enough, multiply? handle via Python SQL union list days
    rows=[]
    # produce per day due exact by day modulo
    due=[]
    for d in range(1,horizon+1): due.append((cur+d,(cur+d)%30))
    cases=' '.join([f"WHEN s.billing_day_mod30={m} THEN {day}" for day,m in due])
    # Cannot handle duplicate mods (for >30) with CASE first only; skip only 7/28 exact below, for 84 estimate repeat 3x mrr weighted maybe.
    if horizon<=28:
        rows=q(f"""
        SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) due
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
        WHERE {active} AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods})
        GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY due DESC
        """)
        print_rows(f'due billings next {horizon}d by group plan', rows, limit=80)
        print_rows(f'due billings next {horizon}d total', q(f"SELECT ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) due FROM subscriptions s WHERE {active} AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({mods})"))
# cohorts around renewal
print_rows('cohorts 174-180 status by group plan', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.start_day BETWEEN 174 AND 180 AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07')
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.status
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('cohorts 181-187 (next renewal) status by group plan', q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.start_day BETWEEN 181 AND 187 AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07')
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.status
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('cohorts 174-180 by start day status', q("""
SELECT s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.start_day BETWEEN 174 AND 180 AND c.group_id IN ('S2','D_S07')
GROUP BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY s.start_day,c.group_id,s.plan,s.status
"""), limit=300)
# recent cancellations by original cohort perhaps
print_rows('cancellations last7 by group plan start range', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, CASE WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN 167 AND 173 THEN '167-173' WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN 174 AND 180 THEN '174-180' WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN 181 AND 187 THEN '181-187' ELSE 'other' END cohort,
       COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>{cur-7} AND s.end_day<= {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,cohort ORDER BY subs DESC
"""), limit=100)
# acquisition last7 by source/group plan/status
print_rows('new small subs/leads created last7 by source group status plan', q(f"""
SELECT c.acquisition_source, c.group_id, COALESCE(s.plan,'') plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(s.effective_price,0)*COALESCE(s.seat_count,0)),2) mrr
FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
WHERE c.created_day>{cur-7} AND c.created_day<= {cur} AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.acquisition_source,c.group_id,plan,s.status ORDER BY n DESC
"""), limit=200)
print_rows('ad leads last7', q(f"SELECT channel_id,group_id,SUM(leads_generated) leads,ROUND(SUM(spend),2) spend, ROUND(SUM(spend)/NULLIF(SUM(leads_generated),0),2) cpl FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>{cur-7} AND day<= {cur} GROUP BY channel_id,group_id ORDER BY spend DESC"), limit=100)
# issues active-only and all
print_rows('open issues active customers by group age', q(f"""
SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) open, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_days, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7, MAX(i.days_open) max_days
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY open DESC
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('open issues all by group age', q("""
SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) open, ROUND(AVG(days_open),2) avg_days, SUM(CASE WHEN days_open>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge2, SUM(CASE WHEN days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7, MAX(days_open) max_days
FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY open DESC
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('issues last7 opened/resolved by group', q(f"""
SELECT group_id,
       SUM(CASE WHEN open_day>{cur-7} AND open_day<= {cur} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) opened,
       SUM(CASE WHEN resolved_day>{cur-7} AND resolved_day<= {cur} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) resolved
FROM issues
WHERE (open_day>{cur-7} AND open_day<= {cur}) OR (resolved_day>{cur-7} AND resolved_day<= {cur})
GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY opened DESC
"""), limit=100)
# usage summary last7 by group plan active? all
print_rows('usage last7 by group plan active', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users, ROUND(SUM(du.usage_units),0) usage
FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=du.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=du.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE du.day>{cur-7} AND du.day<= {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY usage DESC
"""), limit=100)
# Enterprise summary
print_rows('enterprise live subscribed by group plan', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) customers,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY seats DESC
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('enterprise status by group', q("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.status,COUNT(*) customers,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(SUM(COALESCE(s.effective_price,0)*s.seat_count),2) value
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.status ORDER BY c.group_id,s.status
"""), limit=100)
# latest open thread per customer
print_rows('enterprise latest open turn by sender group type day turn', q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid
)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(latest.seat_count) seats
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id
GROUP BY c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number
ORDER BY latest.sender DESC, latest.day DESC, seats DESC
"""), limit=200)
print_rows('enterprise open awaiting non-agent count', q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid
)
SELECT c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(latest.seat_count) seats, MIN(latest.day) min_day, MAX(latest.day) max_day, MIN(latest.turn_number) min_turn, MAX(latest.turn_number) max_turn
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE latest.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.sender ORDER BY seats DESC
"""), limit=100)
print_rows('enterprise closes last7', q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, et.close_reason, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) turns, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, MIN(et.day) min_day, MAX(et.day) max_day
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=1 AND et.day>{cur-7} AND et.day<= {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id, et.close_reason, et.thread_type ORDER BY seats DESC
"""), limit=100)
# sample latest non-agent messages for counters?
print_rows('sample awaiting non-agent messages', q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.maxid
)
SELECT latest.message_id, latest.customer_id, c.group_id, latest.thread_type, latest.turn_number, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.seat_count, SUBSTR(latest.message_text,1,200) msg, latest.offer_json
FROM latest JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=latest.customer_id
WHERE latest.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY latest.day, latest.seat_count DESC LIMIT 50
"""), limit=60)
